Name: keccak.f_permutation_.in
Description: 576-bit input that is absorbed into the Keccak state on acceptance by XORing into the upper portion of the state.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.out
Description: 1600-bit Keccak state updated each cycle when update is asserted and provided to downstream logic as the permutation result.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.in_ready
Description: Indicates availability of input data and, together with calc, determines when the module accepts new data.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.out_ready
Description: Asserted only at the final round to signal that the output state is valid.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.ack
Description: Signals to the upstream that the current input has been consumed in the cycle it is accepted.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.rc1
Description: Round constant generated from i and fed into the round transformation.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.rc2
Description: Second round constant generated from i and used by the round transformation.

Name: keccak.in[63:0]
Description: Carries the 64-bit message segment into the keccak top module, which is padded and permuted to produce the hash.

Name: keccak.in_ready
Description: Indicates to the keccak module that a new 64-bit input chunk is available to be accepted and processed.

Name: keccak.out[511:0]
Description: Presents the 512-bit hash result after permutation and byte reordering.

Name: keccak.out_ready
Description: Indicates when the 512-bit hash on out is valid, driven after internal timing/counter conditions are met.

Name: keccak.f_ack
Description: Acknowledges acceptance of the padded block by the permutation engine, enabling the padder/source to advance.

Name: keccak.f_out_ready
Description: Signals that the permutation engine has produced a valid 1600-bit state output.

Name: keccak.padder_.out
Description: 576-bit buffered, padded message block shifted in 64-bit chunks (v1) and provided to f_permutation when the buffer becomes full.

Name: keccak.padder_.in
Description: 64-bit input data chunk from the user; used directly as v1 when not the last word and contributes to the padded block.

Name: keccak.padder_.in_ready
Description: Handshaking input indicating that an input word is available; used in accept = (~state) & in_ready & (~buffer_full).

Name: keccak.padder_.out_ready
Description: Signals when a 576-bit padded block is ready (wired to buffer_full).

Name: keccak.padder_.f_ack
Description: Acknowledgment from f_permutation; used to clear/hold the buffer length counter i and control when the padder can start filling a new block.

Name: keccak.padder_.done
Description: Set when state=1 and out_ready=1; prevents further buffer updates once a final padded block is produced.

Name: keccak.padder_.p0.in
Description: 64-bit data chunk to be padded; depending on byte_num, the upper bytes of this word are passed through to the output while the next byte is set to 0x01 and the remaining bytes to 0.

Name: keccak.padder_.p0.out
Description: 64-bit padded word combining the valid portion of 'in' with the 0x01 marker and trailing zeros, forwarded to the higher-level padder/buffer.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.rconst_.rc1
Description: 64-bit round constant derived combinationally from input 'i'; used in the round2in1 module as round_const_1 for Keccak’s iota step.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.rconst_.rc2
Description: 64-bit round constant derived combinationally from input 'i'; used in the round2in1 module as round_const_2 for the subsequent iota application within two-round processing.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.round_.in
Description: 1600-bit input state to the two-round Keccak transformation; it is decomposed into 5x5 lanes and feeds theta, rho, pi, chi, and iota steps.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.round_.out
Description: 1600-bit output state after applying two rounds of Keccak (including iota with provided round constants); forwarded to subsequent rounds or modules.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.round_.round_const_1
Description: 64-bit round constant XORed into lane g[0][0] during the iota step of the first round.

Name: keccak.f_permutation_.round_.round_const_2
Description: 64-bit round constant XORed into lane gg[0][0] during the iota step of the second round.
